OVER BLACK: HARD DRIVING ROCK GUITAR RIFF - Bluesy, tough, mean rock n' roll. THE RIFF BUILDS into the TITLE SONG Title Sequence: a MONTAGE of BLACK AND WHITE STILLS depicting "Harry Doyle and Archie Long Strike Again! - Police Stumped!" "Doyle and Long Net Biggest Take Ever!" "Gold Coast Flyer Robbed! Bandits Grab $450,000!" The TITLE SEQUENCE ENDS with this last headline as the MUSIC ENDS and the PICTURE DISSOLVES TO:
INT. ROOM - DAY

IN CLOSE-UP, we SEE the same headline from the LA Times, announcing: "Gold Coast Flyer Robbed! Bandits Grab $450,000!" It is now framed and yellowing. We PAN ACROSS to FIND another--this one announces: "Train Bandits Get 35 Years!" In CLOSE-UP, we SEE a record turning on a turntable. A hand holding the tonearm gently drops the stylus onto the record. We HEAR a SCRATCHY RECORDING of "Don't Get Around Much Anymore" begin to PLAY. In CLOSE-UP, a hand comes INTO FRAME and removes the framed headlines from the wall. We PAN ACROSS AND HOLD on a black and white photograph of Harry Doyle in his early 30's, leaning proudly against a brand-new Cadillac circa 1946. Then the photo is removed from the wall. We PULL BACK TO SEE that we are in a prison cell at the Correctional Facility at Terminal Island, California. HARRY DOYLE, now a dignified man of 73, dressed in prison denims, is standing in a cell, looking fondly at the photo. He takes a billfold from the cot and puts the photo carefully into it. The record starts to SKIP on the lyric " ..* "Don't get around much .*. Don't get around much ... Don't get around much ... ". Harry reaches down and lifts the needle off the record. FROM OFF-CAMERA, we begin to HEAR the sound of HARD DRIVING ROCK MUSIC. In CLOSE-Up, we SEE the face of ARCHIE LONG, now a vital man of 67. His teeth are bared in a vicious grin. His face is beaded with sweat. A towel is draped around his neck. With a GRUNT of effort, Archie curls a heavilyweighted barbell up to his chest in rhythm to the ROCK MUSIC. He exhales and curls the barbell down. We PULL BACK to REVEAL that Archie is also in a prison cell. He is dressed only in sweatpants and a leather back support. We can SEE that Archie is in extremely good shape for a man his age. At one end of his cot, there is a small radio. We CONTINUE PULLING BACK TO REVEAL that Harry and Archie occupy the same prison cell. A white line is painted down the center of the large cell, dividing it exactly in half. Harry's side is neat and tidy: His bed is made, his clothes hang neatly from a steam pipe in the back. Archie's side of the cell is a mess: His bed is unmade, his clothes are balled up in a corner; the walls are plastered with pin-ups. Archie finishes his curls and drops the barbells onto the floor with a CLANG. Harry winces at the sound of the CLANG. Archie takes the towel from around his neck and begins drying himself, constantly moving to the rhythms coming from the radio. As he moves, he turns and SEES Harry packing. He starts across the cell. IN CLOSE-UP: the framed headline, lying on Harry's cot, which reads "Train Bandits Get 35 Years!" Archie's hand comes INTO FRAME and lifts it off the cot. WIDE: Archie stares at the headline and shakes his head remorsefully. Harry is beside him.
(sighing) You know if I had to do it all over again ... (cutting him off) You'd steal the whole damn train and ride it clear to Mexico.
Have I mentioned that before?
Harry looks over at Archie. the headline away from .him. He reaches out and takes
Every day, for 30 years.

Harry puts the headline on his bed. Archie finishes drying himself and absently drops the towel on the floor. Harry leans down, picks it up with two fingers and deposits it on Archie's side of the cell.
PRISON CORRIDOR - DAY

Harry and Archie stand before the PROPERTIES DESK where an impassive GUARD hands each a cardboard box containing his personal effects. The boxes are coated with a thick layer of dus.t which flies as Harry opens his and removes a vintage 1940's Fedora and Timex watch.
We could have been South of the border, livin' like Kings all this time, Harry.
(ignoring Archie while winding his watch) How do you like that, Arch?
Takes a licking and keeps on ticking. Archie sees that Harry is feeling too good to argue right now. He smil.es, picks up his cardboard box and both men exit.
PRISON CORRIDOR - DAY
Harry and Archie walk TOWARDS CAMERA with purpose and determination and in perfect synchronization.
First think I want to do ... is walk on grass. I ain't walked on. grass in thirty years. Then I wanna sit under a tree and
listen to a bird sing.
I wanna party! I wanna bop till I drop! I wanna try Chicken Mc Nuggets! And I wanna hold a woman ... for about five years!
They walk past a YOUNG BLACK CONVICT who is mopping the floor.
You best not be talkin' about holding my old lady, man.
Are youkiddin'? I never paid for it in my life.
The convict laughs *.* they exchange "High Fives" and we
PRISON STAIRWELL - DAX
Harry and Archie descend the staircase.
Respect, Arch, that's the ticket. We're gonna live useful and prqductive lives **. be respected members of the community.

A CONVICT passes and calls out.
Good luck, guys *.. you'll need it.
Archie's smile slowly fades.

But we don't even know where we're gonna sleep tonight!
Probably in a bed! be negative!
Now, don't They turn a corner, still. in*perfect synchronization.
PRISON - DAY

A Buzzer SOUNDS releasing the electric bolt on the door that leads to the prison exterior. Harry and Archie step out into the daylight. They are now dressed in the clothes they wore into the prison thirty years ago. Ironically, their suits, which were spiffy in the fifties are stylish in the eighties *. Each carries a small suitcase.
Yeah.
I can't wait to see the
Think they'll recognize us? Why not?
We ain't changed.
The best things never do.
Damn right!
They come to a stop in front of a heavy barred gate. They look over at the OLDER GUARD who is on the other side. Harry and Archie wait a moment. The Guard doesn't do anything.
You got two men in a hurry to start their new lives. Mind opening this
door.
What the hell for? You're never gonna make it! You guys'll vjiolate your parole in a week!
Correct me if I'm wrong, Arch .*. but do I detect a note of pessimism from this man?
To tell you the truth, I think he's being downright hostile.
The guard LAUGHS derisively. slides it open. He unlocks the door and Archie gestures politely for Harry to go .-first. ARCHIE After you.
Bo, no, after you.
Age before beauty.
(smiling as he walks through the door) Pearls before swine.
Archie smiles: "Touche", then follows Harry through the gate. The guard watches them go.
See you guys in a week!
GUARD'S POV: Archie puts his hand behind his back and gives the guard "the finger".
BUS STOP - DAY Harry and Archie are at a bus stop near the prison,
their suitcases at their sides. Behind them is a row of newspaper boxes. Archie is bending down, trying to read the headlines. Harry takes a deep breath, beaming.
I feel re-born, Arch; Like a
air is free!
So's this newspaper.

Archie glances around quickly to make sure no one is watching, then takes the :tandle on one of the boxes and yanks it open, cleanly and quietly, then pulls out a newspaper. He turns to SEE Harry looking at him sternly.
I just wanted to see if I still had it.
Respectable citizens don't steal newspapers.
Jesus, Harry ..* it's only a lousy two-bits!
That ain't the point. You can't put a price on honesty. Now put a quarter in the box!

Harry takes ~he newspaper from him. Archie sighs and*reaches into his pocket. quarter into the newspaper box. He drops a Harry looks at the front page of the paper. HARRY'S POV: The Headline reads: Retires After 50 Years!" "Gold Coast Flyer
(reading; over headline) Look at this, Arch. "The Gold Coast Flyer retires after SO years".

Doesn't it say anything about us?
Harry skims down the article quickly.
Just says it was robbed. say by whom.
Can you beat that? The last guys in America to rob a train *.* and they don't even mention our names.
Thirty years is a long time. Might be nobody remembers us.
They'd remember us if we'd gotten away with it!
Excuse me.
They both turn and look and SEE a PREPPY-LOOKING YOUNG MAN walking toward them, beaming. YOUNG MAN Harry Doyle *.. and Archie Long? Harry and Archie glance at each other in surprise:
"maybe

Who wants to know? I'm Richie Evans. I've been looking forward to meeting you for a long time.
(puzzled) You've been lookin' forward to meeting' us?

I'm your probation officer.
Harry and Archie's smiles fade. They glance at each other.
(very surprised) Did you say ... probation officer?
Richie nods, smiling.
(straightening up) Well ... it's a ... thrill for us too ** uh ... sir.
Do you always meet parolees right at the prison gate?
Not ordinarily *.. but then you guys aren't ordinary parolees.
We ain't?

Not as far as I'm concerned. You're the best there was! know everything there is to know about you guys. In college, my masters thesis was on "The Great Outlaws of the Twentieth Century" --John Dillinger, Pretty Boy Floyd, Willie Sutton -- all the greats! When I heard you were coming up for probation, I actually asked for your cases!
You did?
Sure, you're the last of your k+-nd.
Harry and Archie start a "routine."
That's all in the past. We're fully rehabilitated now.
That's right *.* We've learned our lesson *.. we don't even think about that stuff anymore!
That's great! Anyway, I thought you guys might need a lift. That's why I came out here. ARCHIE That's ..* uh **. real nice of you ... Mr. Evans.
Call me Richie. You guys wait here. I'll get the car and be right back, okay?
Richie turns and starts off toward the parking lot.
This is a great beginning, Arch.
Things are lookin' up already.

Just then, an old Buick, kicking a cloud of blue smoke from its exhaust pipe, pulls up to the bus stop. The driver climbs out and looks across the car at Harry and Archie. This is LEON ET.KES, 66, wearing a cheap suit and raincoat and very thick-lensed glasses.
Are you two garners Harry Doyle and Archie Long?
Harry and Archie glance at each other again in surprise!
We got a fan club, Harry.
(turning back to
That's us, friend. And who might you be?
I might be the guy's been waitin' thirty years to blow your heads off!

He brings a sawed-off pump shotgun out from under his coat and aims it at Harry and Archie. Harry and Archie drop to the ground, just as Leon FIRES TWICE. The slugs TEAR into one of the newspaper boxes, BLOWING IT TO PIECES a?d sending a SHOWER of quarters onto the pavement. The recoil causes Leon's glasses to fly off his face and CLATTER to the pavement.

Leon begins feeling around on the pavement for his glasses, like a blind man. Harry picks up his suitcase and starts to run. starts picking up the loose quarters.
(turning back)
(still picking up quarters) Okay, okay ...

PARKING LOT - DAY
Harry and Archie are hurrying across the lot, looking around for Richie.
I don't know! before!
I never saw him

Well I ain't about to go.back and ask him!
PARR-ING "LOT - ELSEWHERE - DAY
Richie is standing by his VOLVO, just about to put the key into the lock. Harry and Archie hurry*up beside him.
I was gonna pick you guys up!
(quickly) We thought we'd save you the trip.

(quickly) You don't mind if I drive, do ya?

VOLVO - DAY

EXT. _VOLVO - DAY

Way behind.

6/13(86 - Expiration of Parole 6/13/89"
"Long, Archibald T. - #2338749 - Armed Robbery - PAROLED

room at the Golden Sunset Retirement Home *..

*.* and, as soon as you sign that, $435 a month in Social Security.

be visitin' you all the time, we'll have some laughs maybe go out and pick up some broads ***

PARK - DAY

~nd if I see you around here again, youi11 be doin' your bird watchin' from inside a cage!

DAY

today? How are you

button and it

to move forward. Harry starts

! asked where you were going, man.
